original_hypothesis.title: Landlord Tax Rate Bait-and-Switch Without Automatic Farming of Existing Stakers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: updateTaxRate does not trigger _farmPlots for existing stakers before changing the rate. While the latestTaxRate snapshot in toilerState protects the current accrued period, the rate change is immediately stored and will be applied to the NEXT farming period for all stakers. A landlord can attract renters with a low tax rate, then switch to MAX_TAX_RATE. The new rate takes effect from the renter's next farm call, giving the renter no way to retroactively protect their future accrual.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The code updates plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate directly without farming existing stakers. toilerState[tokenId].latestTaxRate is only updated when the renter farms, creating a window for rate manipulation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate = newTaxRate — direct assignment with no call to _farmPlots for existing stakers on this landlord

preconditions: Landlord has stakers on their plots
Renter farms after the rate change

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Rate change is stored globally while per-token rate is updated lazily on farm, creating a manipulation window
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Effective from rate change until renter reacts by unstaking

operational_questions: How frequently do landlords change tax rates in practice?
What is the maximum economic impact of a bait-and-switch?
Would forcing farming on rate change be gas-prohibitive?
validation_requirements: Test: landlord sets 1% rate, renter stakes and farms, landlord raises to MAX, renter farms again — verify second period uses MAX
Calculate maximum schnibbles lost to renter from a bait-and-switch

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Landlord sets attractive low tax rate to attract renters
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Renters stake and begin farming at low rate
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Renters' next farm applies MAX rate for the entire period since last farm
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Single landlord acting in bad faith

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Unfair tax extraction from renters who relied on advertised rate
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low — renters can unstake but lose expected yield

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Most effective when renters haven't farmed recently

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Rapid tax rate increases on landlords with active stakers
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Landlords cycling between MIN and MAX rates
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Renters unstaking shortly after rate changes

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
